# What a senior recruiter would zero in on

## The bet
Lead with the Qonto SEPA migration. That's the closest analog to Pennylane's payments platform — same domain, same scale of architecture decision, same on-call posture.

## The fear (HM perspective)
Mentorship at Staff level. The CV says "mentored 3 mid-level engineers" but doesn't say what changed about them, what playbook was built, or how the cross-team influence works. At Pennylane the mentorship slice is 20% of the role.

## Cliché killer
"Helped ship the new merchant onboarding API" — at Stripe-level scale, "helped" is undersold. Either Léa owned a meaningful slice or didn't.

## Unspoken evidence
- Stripe Connect → almost certainly touched payment activation / onboarding flows. Pennylane wants exactly that. Worth probing.
- Doctolib recurring-appointment feature scaled to all FR practitioners — implies high-throughput data + scheduling. Distributed-systems adjacency.

## What the chat should NOT do
- Open on "what's your tech stack?" — the CV already lists Ruby/Go/Postgres/Kafka. Wasted slot.
- Treat all three companies as equal weight. 80% of the recruiter's interest is in Qonto + Stripe; Doctolib is supporting context.
